My credit card company Barclays called me on around XX/XX/XXXX about potential fraud charges on my account and they said those charges had been blocked and did not go through. However, they still showed up on my credit card account as valid charges. I called Barclays to dispute those charges, which started on XX/XX/XXXX with multiple small amounts, such as {$1.00}, {$1.00}, and these charges suddently increased to multiple {$200.00}, {$100.00}, {$50.00}, and {$25.00} charges. All these charges were billed by XXXX and were charged to my wife 's credit card number, who is an authorized user under my account. 

I reached out to the merchant XXXX and they told me that those charges were from an XXXX ID that reasonable my XXXX old son 's name. It seems that my son had taken my wife 's credit card and used to purchase various games through XXXX in XXXX and XXXX. 

Please note that my wife 's credit card had not been used for a long time and was inside my drawer. It had been blocked under my Barclays account profilie. I do not understand how these charges could still be charged to this credit card number. 

Also if my XXXX old son misused the card to make online purchase, it was done without our permission and approval. We should not be liable for these charges.
